subtopic: Strategic Projects and Services Sector Trends
title: Strategic Projects and Services Sector Trends
focus: Groups strategic rail/tunnel projects (USBRL, Chenab) with NITI Aayog's insights on GVA and employment trends in the services sector.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 074]
9.7. INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS
| Project Name | Strategic Significance & Key Features |
| :--- | :--- |
| Udhampur-Srinagar-Baramulla Rail Link (USBRL) (Kashmir Link) | • Integration: Definitive rail link for Kashmir Valley to rest of India.\n• Engineering: Features World’s highest rail bridge (Chenab). |
| Chenab Bridge | • Engineering Marvel: World's highest railway arch bridge (359m above river bed, 35m higher than Eiffel Tower).\n• Strategic Link: Critical component of the USBRL, enabling all-weather troop and logistics mobility to the Kashmir Valley. |
| Bairabi-Sairang Rail Link | • Capital Connectivity: Connects Aizawl (Mizoram) to the national railway grid.\n• Act East Policy: Facilitates trade with Southeast Asia via the upcoming Kaladan Multi-Modal Transit Transport Project. |
| Sonamargh (Z-Morh) Tunnel | • Strategic: All-weather access to Sonamarg.\n• Defense: Crucial for troop mobility to Ladakh region. |

[Page 075]
10. SERVICES
10.1. INDIA’S SERVICES SECTOR
India’s Services Sector
NITI Aayog released two reports titled as Insights from GVA trends and State-level dynamics and Insights from Employment trends and State-level dynamics.
Key Highlights of the Reports
Services at the Core of India’s Employment Transition
- It employed 188 million workers in 2023-24, and is the second-largest employer.
- In 2024-25, the services sector contributed nearly 55% of Gross Value Added (GVA), while the primary and secondary sectors accounted for 16% and 29%, respectively.
- Despite a high contribution to GVA, it provided less than one-third of total jobs (mostly informal and low-paying).
Employment Landscape
- Added ~40 million jobs in six years, second only to construction.
- Acts as a labour shock absorber, but is divided between:
- High-value services (IT, finance, healthcare, professional services), productive but limited employment.
- Traditional services (trade, transport), major employers, yet highly informal.
Employment Profile
- Spatial: 60% of urban workers, as compared to less than 20% of rural workers in services.
- Gender: Only 10.5% of rural women are in services as compared to 60% of urban women.
- Informality: 87% lack social security; rural women earn less than 50% of men’s wages.
UPPSC Mains Test Series & Mentoring Program 2026
VisionIAS UPSC expertise now for UPPCS aspirants. UPSC-style Test Series, Evaluation, and Mentoring now designed for UPPCS preparation. Available in Hindi & English. Admission Open.
